pathophysiology_summary: Post-viral autonomic dysregulation impairs compensatory vasoconstriction on standing, causing venous pooling; the heart rate rises excessively (>30 bpm) to maintain cerebral perfusion, producing pre-syncope, tunnel vision and fatigue that resolve on lying flat. Blood pressure is maintained (distinguishing POTS from orthostatic hypotension), and hydration is adequate, arguing against severe hypovolaemia. The dramatic tachycardia and near-collapse mimic cardiogenic syncope/arrhythmia and PE, but the strictly postural pattern, immediate settling on recumbency, absence of rest palpitations/chest pain and normal oxygen point to a POTS-pattern dysautonomia. Telehealth cannot reliably measure postural vitals, auscultate, or perform an ECG, so an arrhythmia cannot be fully excluded remotely and the high fall risk must be managed; the correct action is supervised supportive care plus prompt in-person review with an ECG, not remote initiation of rate-control drugs.

differential.evidence_basis: Postural tachycardia with pre-syncope on a post-viral background is most likely.
Tachycardia and near-collapse require ECG to exclude arrhythmia.
Volume depletion can cause postural symptoms; screen hydration and BP.
Post-COVID prothrombotic state; screen for chest pain/hypoxia.
Postural-only tachycardia, settling flat, no rest palpitations, BP maintained, adequate hydration — POTS phenotype, pending ECG.
Lower probability but not excludable without ECG/Holter.
No chest pain/hypoxia; racing settles immediately lying flat.
Working telehealth diagnosis: POTS-pattern orthostatic intolerance; confirm with in-person ECG/postural vitals, manage fall risk, and support with fluids/salt/counter-manoeuvres.

red_flags.description: Palpitations/tachycardia occurring at rest lying flat, or persisting >120 bpm supine
red_flags.clinical_significance: Rest tachycardia suggests a primary arrhythmia rather than POTS and needs urgent ECG.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Missed arrhythmia with risk of cardiac syncope.

red_flags.description: True loss of consciousness (syncope) or injurious fall
red_flags.clinical_significance: True syncope broadens the differential (cardiac) and signals significant injury risk.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Head injury/fracture from an unwitnessed collapse.

red_flags.description: Chest pain or breathlessness with the episodes
red_flags.clinical_significance: Would raise cardiac/PE causes requiring emergency assessment.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Diagnosing POTS without arranging an ECG to exclude arrhythmia.
Starting beta-blockers/ivabradine over chat before diagnosis/ECG, risking hypotension.
Not addressing the high fall/injury risk and reliance on the parent for safe transfers.
Recommending very high salt/fluid loading without checking for contraindications (e.g. renal/cardiac).

risk_scores.expected_score_or_tier: Sustained HR rise >30 bpm on standing without orthostatic hypotension (POTS criterion), reproducible on a supervised home stand
risk_scores.clinical_implication: Supports a POTS-pattern diagnosis and conservative management with ECG confirmation.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Reliable clinician-measured supine/standing vitals and cardiac auscultation
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Provide supervised fall-safety and supportive measures (fluids/salt within limits, counter-manoeuvres), arrange prompt in-person review with an ECG, and escalate to 000/ED for true syncope, chest pain, or tachycardia persisting while lying flat.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Starting rate-control drugs remotely or reassuring without an ECG could miss an arrhythmia and precipitate hypotension/falls.

guidelines_referenced.reference: Heart Rhythm Society expert consensus on POTS
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: POTS is defined by a sustained HR increase >30 bpm on standing without orthostatic hypotension; management begins with fluids, salt, exercise and counter-manoeuvres, with medications reserved and specialist-guided.
Syncope/pre-syncope warrants ECG and assessment to exclude arrhythmic/structural causes before attribution to a benign cause.
